Record Class BackPackView

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
fr.uge.view.BackPackView
Record Components:
yOrigin - The screen Y coordinate where the grid starts.
width - The total width of the view area.
height - The total height of the view area.
tileSize - The size in pixels of a single grid square.
loader - The image loader.
public record BackPackView(int xOrigin, int yOrigin, int width, int height, int tileSize, ImageLoader loader) extends Record
Handles the graphical presentation of the player's Backpack (Inventory).

This view draws the grid, the items placed within it, the bottom item dock, and handles the rendering of items currently being dragged (floating).

* @param xOrigin The screen X coordinate where the grid starts.

  • Constructor Details

    • BackPackView

      public BackPackView(int xOrigin, int yOrigin, int width, int height, int tileSize, ImageLoader loader)
      Compact constructor with validation. * @param xOrigin X start.
      Parameters:
      yOrigin - Y start.
      width - View width.
      height - View height.
      tileSize - Size of a tile.
      loader - The image loader.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if loader is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if dimensions or tileSize are not positive.

  • Method Details

    • create

      public static BackPackView create(com.github.forax.zen.ApplicationContext ctx, ImageLoader loader)
      Factory method to create a centered BackPackView based on the current screen info.
      Parameters:
      ctx - The application context.
      loader - The image loader.
      Returns:
      A new instance of BackPackView centered on the screen.

    • draw

      public static void draw(com.github.forax.zen.ApplicationContext ctx, BackPack bp, BackPackView view, Item held, Coordinate cur, List<Item> dock)
      Static entry point to render the Backpack interface.
      Parameters:
      ctx - The application context for graphics rendering.
      bp - The backpack model containing items.
      view - The view settings (dimensions, origin).
      held - The item currently held by the mouse cursor (floating), or null.
      cur - The current grid coordinate under the mouse cursor.
      dock - The list of items available in the bottom dock.

    • toCoordinate

      public Coordinate toCoordinate(float x, float y)
      Converts screen mouse coordinates into Backpack grid coordinates.
      Parameters:
      x - The mouse X position on the screen.
      y - The mouse Y position on the screen.
      Returns:
      The corresponding coordinate in the backpack grid.

    • isCursorInDock

      public boolean isCursorInDock(float y)
      Checks if the mouse Y position is within the bottom dock area.
      Parameters:
      y - The mouse Y position.
      Returns:
      true if the cursor is over the dock.

    • getItemAtDock

      public Item getItemAtDock(float x, float y, List<Item> items)
      Retrieves the item located at the specific screen coordinates within the dock.
      Parameters:
      x - The mouse X position.
      y - The mouse Y position.
      items - The list of items currently in the dock.
      Returns:
      The item under the cursor, or null if none found.

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. Reference components are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object); primitive components are compared with the compare method from their corresponding wrapper classes.
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
